title: echium lusitanicum subsp. polycaulon
intro: Echium lusitanicum subsp. polycaulon, also known as the Portuguese bugloss, is a subspecies of flowering plant within the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been reported to exhibit hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including antioxidant and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and support the digestive system. Echium lusitanicum subsp. polycaulon is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, which are typically consumed to harness its medicinal properties and purported health benefits.
